content:
===BEGIN===
Vaccines work like a training course for your body’s defense system, known as the immune system. When you receive a vaccine, it introduces a harmless piece or a weakened form of a virus or bacteria. This doesn’t cause illness but teaches your immune system to recognize and respond quickly to the real threat in the future. Think of it as a practice drill that prepares your body to fight infection more effectively. By “remembering” the invader, your immune system can act fast to prevent sickness or reduce its severity. This training helps protect not only the individual but also the wider community by slowing the spread of disease. In short, vaccines empower your immune system to defend you proactively, saving time, health, and resources in the long run.
